title: How to Find Funders and Partners
description: Webinar organized by Ghana chapter to educate others on how to find funders and partners for their activities.

title: UK Internet Governance Forum 2025
description: One-day event in London focused on “Building Digital Governance Together,” exploring key issues like digital cooperation, trust, innovation, and universal digital rights to shape the future of internet governance in the UK

title: ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize
description: Delivery of the ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize and panel discussion on the Age Control proposals

title: Universal Acceptance on a Multilingual Internet
description: This webinar will explore how Universal Acceptance forms the foundation for a truly multilingual Internet, the challenges of regional language support, multilingual DNS, inclusion in digital platforms, and the transformative role of technology, design, and policy in shaping linguistic equity online.

title: AI SIG "Wire-Free Sewer Vision" Hybrid Hackathon
description: A critical event designed to challenge how we monitor and maintain underground sewer systems using cutting-edge, autonomous technology.
organizer: ISOC Artificial Intelligence (AI) Special Interest Group

title: LINUX Para Uso Cotidiano
description: Este seminario introduce a Linux como una herramienta útil para el uso cotidiano. Se presentará el trasfondo operativo del sistema, las diferencias entre diversas distribuciones y cómo obtenerlas e instalarlas en distintas plataformas. Incluye demostraciones prácticas del entorno Linux y una sesión interactiva de preguntas y respuestas para aclarar dudas y guiar a quienes deseen comenzar a utilizar este sistema operativo en su vida diaria.

title: Budding Polymath
description: An intra-chapter event which showcases our young talent presenting on relevant topics

title: Meta-Layer Design Challenge
description: The Meta-Layer Design Challenge is a global, week-long design challenge exploring how learning, collaboration, and collective intelligence can be supported above the existing Web through a shared civic interface layer - the Meta-Layer. Rather than building new platforms, participants are invited to design concepts, interfaces, and governance models that operate across websites using overlays, shared context, trust signals, and community coordination. The challenge emphasizes human-centered design, interoperability, and public-interest outcomes. The Design Challenge is part of Meta-Layer Week (January 21–28, 2025), co-hosted with Learning Planet. Activities include live sessions, workshops, demonstrations, office hours, and a final showcase. Participants may submit conceptual designs, mockups, diagrams, governance proposals, or lightweight prototypes. No coding is required. Both a Student Track and an Open Track are offered, with shared judging criteria focused on creativity, feasibility, and relevance to collective learning and public-interest infrastructure. The challenge is relevant to Internet governance, digital public infrastructure, education, and community-driven innovation, and aligns with long-standing Internet Society interests in an open, trustworthy, and people-centered Internet.  Audience: Students, educators, designers, technologists, researchers, and community organizers
organizer: ISOC US San Francisco Bay Area Chapter

title: Art by the Internet
description: A talk by DY Kim

title: What does Digital Sovereignty mean for the Civil Society?
description: ISOC Switzerland Chapter’s yearly Public Policy Sessions event will be dedicated in 2026 on the concept of digital sovereignty, launching a long term campaign asking the question “What does digital sovereignty mean?” from the perspective of different actors. As a US-based organization defending digital rights across the world since the 90s, the Internet Society’s European Chapters can play a key role in making sure that the current wave of European claims on digital sovereignty will strengthen digital rights worldwide, while taking into account important challenges related to ecological sustainability, health, and resilience and key values like openness, privacy, and democracy. We are proud to welcome in Zurich 5 special guests with strong expertise on this topic from an academic and/or policy perspective, and active engagement with various civil society organizations, like ISOC Germany and Serbia, FSFE, Wikimedia, La Quadrature du Net. You will have also the chance to meet the growing ISOC Switzerland Chapter and its new home at the center of Zurich, the L200 space, and participate in the discussion!

title: South School on Internet Governance 2026
description: The South School on Internet Governance trains the next generation of thought leaders in all aspects of Internet Governance, with a global perspective and a special focus on Latin America and the Caribbean. The program prepares university and postgraduate students to navigate the complexities of Internet Governance, increase regional representation in international forums, and become active leaders in shaping the future of the Internet in their countries and across the region.

title: Universal Acceptance Day 2026- Regional Event
description: Register Idea Universal Acceptance is a global initiative focused on promoting the internationalization of national domain names (IDNs) and the email addresses associated with them (EAIs).

title: Fundamentos de IA Aplicada a Redes- Mayo
description: Este curso se divide en 4 secciones. Aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda con datos geoespaciales, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: IA para Planificación y Diseño de Redes- Junio
description: El curso tiene 4 secciones donde aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Uso Correcto de la Tecnología Digital
description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción práctica y accesible sobre cómo utilizar la tecnología digital de forma segura, responsable y eficiente.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Lenguajes de Programación de Computadoras
description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción clara y estructurada a los fundamentos de los lenguajes de programación, dirigido a quienes están interesados en comprender cómo funcionan las herramientas que impulsan el desarrollo de software.
organizer: ISOC Puerto Rico Chapter en colaboración con el Colegio de Ingenieros de Puerto Rico

title: Celebración del 30 Aniversario del Capítulo México
description: El solemne acto de celebración del trigésimo aniversario del Capítulo México de la Internet Society abordará con los propios pioneros cómo se dio origen al primer backbone para el Internet Mexicano, además se reconocerá la trayectoria y aportes de los pioneros

title: Semana del Día de Internet
description: Del 18 al 23 de mayo de 2026, coordinamos una serie de eventos conmemorativos del Día de Internet en diversas sedes de la ciudad, incluyendo universidades y centros de eventos, abordando temáticas clave como Seguridad, Gobernanza y Conectividad

title: UA TechBoost 2026 LACNOG
description: UA TechBoost es una iniciativa estratégica enmarcada en el UA Day que busca impulsar la Aceptación Universal como pilar de un internet inclusivo y multilingüe. El evento reúne a líderes de ICANN, UNESCO, ISOC y LACNOG para coordinar la adopción de estándares que permitan reconocer nombres de dominio y correos electrónicos en cualquier idioma o escritura. Para América Latina, representa un puente clave entre la comunidad técnica y el sector académico, asegurando que la transformación digital de la región no deje a nadie atrás por barreras lingüísticas.
organizer: LACNOG, ISOC Venezuela Chapter y VENOG

title: Webinar sobre el proyecto MEDICIONES LAC
description: Un proyecto colaborativo para entender mediciones en Internet en LAC y trabajar en la implementación del DNSSEC

title: Grupo de Mediciones LAC - ISOC Uruguay Chapter
description: Webinar organizado por Capítulo ISOC Uruguay para presentar avances del grupo a la comunidad regional

title: Legislación de menores en redes sociales Caso: Costa Rica
description: Análisis sobre verificación de edad y la nueva legislación propuesta en Costa Rica para el control de uso de redes sociales en menores de edad.

title: ARGENSIG - Argentina School on Internet Governance
description: ARGENSIG is based in Argentina but welcomes fellows from across Latin America, both onsite and hybrid. Its central objective is to train future internet governance leaders, covering the technical, political, economic, social, and legal dimensions of the digital ecosystem, combining a global perspective with a focus on the specific challenges of Argentina and the region.

title: South School on Internet Governance (SSIG) Guatemala 2026
description: The South School on Internet Governance (SSIG) Guatemala 2026 is a capacity-building program designed to strengthen knowledge and skills on Internet governance among participants from Guatemala and the Latin America and Caribbean region. The program will address key topics such as Internet governance processes, multistakeholder participation, cybersecurity, Internet infrastructure, digital policy, and emerging technologies. This initiative promotes regional collaboration, technical and policy capacity development, and active participation in national and international Internet governance processes.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Centro de Capacitación para América Latina y el Caribe (CCATLAT), and Superintendencia de Telecomunicaciones (SIT)

title: Día Internacional de las Niñas en las TIC 2026 – Guatemala
description: The International Girls in ICT Day 2026 in Guatemala aims to inspire and empower girls to explore careers in technology. The event will feature keynote presentations, interactive sessions, and discussions on artificial intelligence, digital skills, and the role of girls in shaping the digital future, promoting STEM education and digital inclusion across Latin America and the Caribbean.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Superintendencia de Telecomunicaciones (SIT) y Ministerio de Comunicaciones, Infraestructura y Vivienda (MICIVI)

title: Guatemala Internet Governance Forum 2026 (IGF Guatemala 2026)
description: The Guatemala Internet Governance Forum (IGF Guatemala 2026) is a national multistakeholder platform bringing together government, academia, the private sector, civil society, and the technical community to discuss key internet governance issues. Topics include digital policy, cybersecurity, infrastructure, connectivity, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. The forum promotes open dialogue and collaboration to support a secure, resilient, and inclusive internet ecosystem in Guatemala and across Latin America and the Caribbean.
organizer: ISOC Guatemala Chapter, Guatemala Internet Governance Forum- Multistakeholder Community
